questions.q: Ram Prasad Bismil was a leading figure of which revolutionary organization?
questions.explanation: Bismil was a leading member of the Hindustan Republican Association (HRA).

questions.q: Bismil was a key participant in which famous train robbery?
questions.explanation: Bismil led the Kakori train robbery of 1925.

questions.q: Apart from being a revolutionary, Bismil was also known as a what?
questions.explanation: Bismil was a celebrated poet, hence his title 'the poet-revolutionary'.

questions.q: With which stirring patriotic verse is Bismil popularly associated?
questions.explanation: Bismil is closely associated with the verse 'Sarfaroshi ki Tamanna'.

questions.q: Bismil was executed at which jail?
questions.explanation: Bismil was hanged at Gorakhpur jail.

questions.q: In which month and year was Bismil executed?
questions.explanation: Bismil was executed in December 1927 for his role in the Kakori case.

questions.q: 'Bismil' was actually what kind of name for Ram Prasad?
questions.explanation: 'Bismil' was his poetic pen name or takhallus, meaning 'the wounded one'.

questions.q: Bismil and his comrades looted the Kakori train in order to obtain funds for what?
questions.explanation: The Kakori loot was meant to finance the purchase of arms for the revolutionary struggle.

questions.q: Bismil belonged to the broad category of Indian freedom fighters who believed in which method?
questions.explanation: Bismil was a revolutionary who believed in armed struggle against British rule.

questions.q: Bismil was born in which present-day state of India?
questions.explanation: Bismil was born at Shahjahanpur in present-day Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: In which year was Ram Prasad Bismil born?
questions.explanation: Ram Prasad Bismil was born on 11 June 1897 at Shahjahanpur.

questions.q: Before Kakori, Bismil was associated with which earlier revolutionary case of the Mainpuri region?
questions.explanation: Bismil was involved in the Mainpuri Conspiracy of 1918 as a young revolutionary.

questions.q: On what date was Bismil hanged at Gorakhpur jail?
questions.explanation: Bismil was executed on 19 December 1927 at Gorakhpur jail.

questions.q: Bismil wrote in multiple languages and used several pen names. Besides 'Bismil', which other pen name did he use?
questions.explanation: He wrote under pen names including 'Ram' and 'Agyat' in addition to 'Bismil'.

questions.q: Which close comrade of Bismil was hanged at Faizabad jail in the same Kakori case?
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan, Bismil's close comrade, was hanged at Faizabad jail in December 1927.

questions.q: Bismil was deeply influenced in his youth by which reform movement that shaped his early ideals?
questions.explanation: Bismil was strongly influenced by the Arya Samaj and the writings of Swami Dayananda Saraswati.

questions.q: While awaiting execution, Bismil wrote an account of his life often described as what?
questions.explanation: Bismil wrote his autobiography in prison before his execution.

questions.q: Bismil's hometown of Shahjahanpur was a centre of activity for which organization he helped lead?
questions.explanation: Shahjahanpur was a key HRA base, and Bismil organised much revolutionary activity from there.

questions.q: Although 'Sarfaroshi ki Tamanna' is strongly linked to Bismil, the verse itself is generally credited to which poet?
questions.explanation: The ghazal 'Sarfaroshi ki Tamanna' was written by Bismil Azimabadi; Ram Prasad Bismil popularised it among revolutionaries.

questions.q: Bismil's revolutionary career began in earnest when he joined a network influenced by which earlier figure connected to the Mainpuri area?
questions.explanation: Bismil worked closely with Genda Lal Dixit in the early Mainpuri-area revolutionary network.

questions.q: Bismil also translated and authored revolutionary literature; one such book promoting self-sacrifice for the nation was titled what?
questions.explanation: Bismil wrote and translated several works, including 'Catherine', inspired by revolutionary themes of liberty.

questions.q: Bismil belonged to which broad ideological strand of the freedom struggle in the 1920s?
questions.explanation: Bismil was a revolutionary nationalist who advocated armed action to win complete independence.

questions.q: Bismil was one of the founding figures of the Hindustan Republican Association, formed in which year?
questions.explanation: The HRA was founded in 1924, with Bismil among its key organisers.

questions.q: Bismil's first brush with revolutionary conspiracy, the Mainpuri Conspiracy, dates to which year?
questions.explanation: The Mainpuri Conspiracy in which Bismil was implicated took place in 1918.

questions.q: Bismil escaped conviction in the Mainpuri Conspiracy partly because he did what?
questions.explanation: Bismil avoided arrest in the Mainpuri case by going underground until a general amnesty was declared.

questions.q: Bismil's autobiography, completed shortly before his death, is most commonly known by which title?
questions.explanation: Bismil's prison autobiography is generally known as his Atmakatha, a candid memoir of his revolutionary life.

questions.q: In the Kakori case verdict, what was the principal sentence handed to Bismil?
questions.explanation: Bismil was sentenced to death by hanging for leading the Kakori conspiracy.

questions.q: Bismil wrote much of his poetry in Hindi and which other language, reflecting the composite culture of his region?
questions.explanation: Bismil composed verse in both Hindi and Urdu, a reflection of the shared literary culture of the United Provinces.

questions.q: Appeals and a mercy petition were made on Bismil's behalf before his hanging; these were ultimately what?
questions.explanation: The appeals and clemency pleas for Bismil were rejected, and the death sentence was carried out.

questions.q: Bismil is remembered for inspiring younger revolutionaries; which later HSRA figure he had once worked alongside became famous in the Lahore conspiracy?
questions.explanation: Chandrashekhar Azad, a Kakori-era associate, went on to lead the reorganised HSRA after Bismil's execution.
